Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 956,699.99 was moved across the accounts in this case.
  • YASH DUBEY sent out Rs 323,317.00, which was the largest source of funds.
  • Money was routed through 1 intermediary account.
  • VIKRAM SAXENA spread money to 79 different accounts.
  • VIKRAM SAXENA collected money from 47 different accounts.
  • Rs 42,312,568.00 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals and Rs 205,099.00 could not be fully traced.
  • The account to investigate first is PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION.

4 · Findings by Account

PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION strong
Account Number 18306700003 · IDFC First Bank · Score 1,055
Rank #1

The account PRIME DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION (18306700003) exhibits suspicious money behavior, with a large credit of 2000000.00 traced into 6 subsequent debits, and multiple patterns of potentially illicit activity. This account has a high score of 1055 and strong patterns, indicating a high risk of financial irregularity. The presence of extreme credits, near-equal debit-credit pairs, and round-value debits further contributes to the suspicious nature of this account.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of 2000000.00 was traced into 6 subsequent debits, with the entire amount allocated and none remaining untraced, indicating a potentially suspicious money trail. (+9 more of this type)
  • Failed Reversed Transaction Detection weak — A debit of 500000.0 was followed by a near-equal credit of 500000.0 with explicit reversal evidence, suggesting a potentially failed reversed transaction.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph was constructed with 156 nodes and 343 directed transaction edges, indicating a complex pattern of money flow.
  • Reversal Clusters weak — The account has 5 debit-reversal pairs, indicating systematic transaction reversal activity, which may be suspicious.
  • Round Value Debit Patterns weak — The account made 63 suspicious round-value debits totaling 14936000.00, with repeated amounts, which could indicate a pattern of illicit transactions.
Assessment: 10 strong pattern type(s) and 4 distinct pattern(s) — strong evidence.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
